call_sequence: STEP 1 (recon): Recover ABI or selectors from BSCScan source or transaction calldata
STEP 2 (recon): Check EIP-1967 proxy slot for implementation address
STEP 3 (recon): Identify any public withdraw(), sweep(), claim(), redeem(), or rescue() selector
STEP 4 (conditional proof): If such a selector exists with no caller check, call it with attacker address as recipient
attacker_path_hypothesis.expected_gain: Up to ~$12.28M in stablecoins if a public value-moving function with no access control exists — unconfirmed pending live context
preconditions: Contract holds ~$12.28M in stablecoins confirmed by chain scanner
Source code and selectors are unknown — hypothesis is conditional on live context
No access control or weak access control on a withdraw/sweep/claim function

live_context_required.name: source_code_and_abi
live_context_required.reason: verified_source is null — no ABI, no function signatures, no selectors are known. Without this, no value-moving path can be identified.

live_context_required.name: transaction_calldata_for_selector_recovery
live_context_required.reason: If source is unverified, recent transaction methodIds can recover public selectors used by depositors, admins, and withdrawal callers.

live_context_required.name: eip1967_proxy_slot_check
live_context_required.reason: Contract may be a proxy. If implementation slot is populated, the real logic and selectors live elsewhere and may have been recently changed.

live_context_required.name: deployer_address_and_funding_trace
live_context_required.reason: deployer_address is empty in the brief. Deployer identity is needed to check for closed-project cluster links, which would elevate score and change next_action to investigate_redeploy_funding_cluster.

live_context_required.name: token_balance_breakdown
live_context_required.reason: Tags confirm stablecoin_balance and known_asset_balance but do not identify which tokens, amounts, or depositors. Needed to confirm live custody and identify the depositor set.

live_context_required.name: owner_admin_role_check
live_context_required.reason: No owner or admin is known. If owner is an EOA with no timelock, admin-triggered extraction is a separate risk class; if there is no owner, public access control gaps become the primary surface.

expected_assertions: contract stablecoin balance decreases by matching amount
no revert due to access control

source_or_selector_basis.evidence: verified_source is null; no transaction methodIds present in brief; no ABI available
source_or_selector_basis.why_it_matters: Without selectors, it is impossible to determine whether any public value-moving path exists. This is the primary blocker for all exploit hypotheses.

source_or_selector_basis.evidence: discovery_source tag is token_funded_contract, meaning the scanner found this contract as a recipient of large ERC20 transfers, not via contract creation scan
source_or_selector_basis.why_it_matters: Confirms real stablecoin value is held. The funding mechanism (ERC20 transfer) is consistent with a deposit vault, escrow, or distributor — all high-value exploit surfaces if access control is absent.

suspected_exploit_families: missing access control on value-moving functions
phantom accounting where shares or debt are created without matching assets
proxy or implementation mismatch that exposes live funds
why_this_target_matters: Fresh BSC contract holding ~$12.28M in stablecoins (token_funded_contract, stablecoin_balance, known_asset_balance). No source verification status, no deployer, no selectors, and no transaction history are present in the brief. The value is real and large; the exploit surface is entirely unknown. The pipeline routed this to recon_bravo_then_corecritical rather than reverse_engineer_unverified_funded_contract because verified_source is null (not explicitly False) and entity_type is unknown_protocol (not unverified_contract), so the unverified-contract scoring branch was not triggered. Live context is required before any exploit hypothesis can be formed.
